persons.name: Arthur Hastings
persons.notes: Narrator of the novel

persons.name: Evelyn Howard
persons.notes: Appeared to be Emily's loyal friend; was Alfred's secret accomplice and lover

deaths.cause_detail: Strychnine introduced into Emily's bromide sleeping powder by Alfred Inglethorp and Evelyn Howard; the drug reacted with a tonic Emily was also taking to produce delayed fatal convulsions
deaths.motive_detail: Emily had made a will leaving her estate to Alfred; he and Evelyn Howard conspired to hasten her death
deaths.notes: Evelyn Howard was Alfred's secret accomplice; the delayed reaction of the poison created a mystery about timing

persons.name: Jack Renauld
persons.notes: In love with Marthe Daubreuil; confessed to killing father to protect Marthe

persons.name: Madame Daubreuil
persons.notes: Also known as Jeanne Beroldy; was blackmailing Renauld about his past

persons.name: Paul Renauld
persons.notes: Real name Georges Conneau; had a criminal past in Canada

deaths.cause_detail: Stabbed and placed in a makeshift grave on the golf course near his home; initially complicated by the appearance of a second body and multiple false confessions
deaths.motive_detail: Renauld was attempting to escape Daubreuil's blackmail; she killed him when the plan to fake his own death failed
deaths.notes: The case involves a second unidentified female body and a false confession by Jack Renauld

persons.name: Dr. James Sheppard
persons.notes: The narrator of the novel; revealed to be the killer in the famous twist

persons.name: Mrs. Ferrars
persons.notes: Died before the novel begins; had been blackmailed by Dr. Sheppard

persons.name: Ralph Paton
persons.notes: Ackroyd's stepson; primary suspect due to disappearance and secret marriage

deaths.cause_detail: Took a fatal overdose of veronal the night before the main action; had been poisoned — or driven to suicide — having confessed to murdering her own husband and been blackmailed by Dr. Sheppard
deaths.motive_detail: Could no longer bear the blackmail; had confessed to Ackroyd in a letter
deaths.notes: Backstory death; she had also killed her first husband (Mr. Ferrars) with arsenic

deaths.cause_detail: Stabbed in the neck with an ornamental Tunisian dagger while alone in his locked study after reading Mrs. Ferrars's confession letter
deaths.motive_detail: Ackroyd had just read Ferrars's letter naming Sheppard as her blackmailer; Sheppard killed him before he could act on the information
deaths.notes: The narrator of the novel is the killer — one of crime fiction's most famous twists

books.title: The Big Four
books.creator: Agatha Christie
books.notes: More spy thriller than classic detective novel; episodic structure adapted from short stories

persons.name: Abe Ryland
persons.notes: Number Two of the Big Four

persons.name: Li Chang Yen
persons.notes: Leader of the Big Four international crime syndicate

persons.name: Madame Olivier
persons.notes: Number Three of the Big Four

deaths.cause_detail: Killed in the explosion that destroys the Big Four's underground headquarters
deaths.motive_detail: Poirot destroyed the headquarters to eliminate the criminal organisation
deaths.notes: Multiple Big Four operatives die across the episodic narrative

deaths.cause_detail: Killed in the destruction of the Big Four's headquarters
deaths.motive_detail: Elimination of the Big Four syndicate

books.title: The Mystery of the Blue Train
books.creator: Agatha Christie

persons.name: Ada Mason
persons.notes: Real name Kitty Kidd; working with jewel thief the Comte de la Roche's associate

persons.name: Derek Kettering
persons.notes: Suspect but ultimately not the killer

persons.name: Mirelle
persons.notes: Derek Kettering's mistress

persons.name: Ruth Kettering
persons.notes: Daughter of millionaire Rufus Van Aldin; travelling to Riviera

deaths.cause_detail: Strangled in her private compartment on the Blue Train overnight; her jewels (the Heart of Fire rubies) stolen; her face battered to delay identification
deaths.motive_detail: Ada Mason and her accomplice killed Ruth to steal the valuable Heart of Fire ruby necklace

persons.name: Freddie Rice
persons.notes: Nick's drug-addicted friend; unwitting pawn

persons.name: Maggie Buckley
persons.notes: Nick's cousin; killed by mistake while wearing Nick's shawl

persons.name: Nick Buckley
persons.notes: Staged multiple apparent attempts on her own life to create an alibi for murder

deaths.cause_detail: Shot at a fireworks party while wearing Nick Buckley's shawl; the killer intended to shoot Nick
deaths.motive_detail: Nick wanted to inherit from her cousin Michael Seton; she needed to appear as a near-murder victim while actually eliminating the real beneficiary
deaths.notes: Twist: the apparent victim (Nick) was actually the killer staging attempts on her own life

persons.name: Carlotta Adams
persons.notes: Hired to impersonate Jane Wilkinson; killed to silence her

persons.name: Jane Wilkinson
persons.notes: The real killer despite a seemingly perfect alibi

persons.name: Lord Edgware
persons.notes: Refused to grant Jane Wilkinson a divorce

persons.name: Ronald Marsh
persons.notes: Lord Edgware's nephew; stood to inherit the title

deaths.cause_detail: Stabbed in the neck in his study; Jane Wilkinson entered disguised as herself (having arranged for Carlotta Adams to impersonate her at a dinner party across town to establish an alibi)
deaths.motive_detail: Jane wanted to be free to marry an American duke; Lord Edgware refused to divorce her

deaths.cause_detail: Found dead from a veronal overdose, staged as accidental; she had been paid to impersonate Jane Wilkinson and had to be silenced
deaths.motive_detail: Carlotta could identify Jane as the one who hired her to create the false alibi

persons.name: Daisy Armstrong
persons.notes: Kidnapped and murdered by Cassetti/Ratchett; her death set the entire revenge plot in motion

persons.name: Hector MacQueen
persons.notes: Son of D.A. MacQueen who was ruined by the Armstrong case

persons.name: Mary Debenham
persons.notes: Was governess in the Armstrong household

persons.name: Princess Dragomiroff
persons.notes: Godmother of Sonia Armstrong

persons.name: Samuel Ratchett
persons.notes: Real name Cassetti; kidnapped and caused the death of Daisy Armstrong

persons.name: multiple
persons.notes: Twelve passengers collectively stabbed Ratchett, each delivering one blow.

deaths.cause_detail: Three-year-old kidnapped and murdered by Cassetti for ransom; death occurred years before the main action
deaths.motive_detail: Ransom kidnapping that ended in the child's murder
deaths.notes: Backstory death; the motive for the entire revenge plot on the Orient Express

deaths.cause_detail: Stabbed twelve times in his locked sleeping compartment overnight while the snowbound train was halted; each of the twelve passengers connected to the Armstrong case dealt one blow
deaths.motive_detail: Collective revenge by twelve people connected to the Armstrong kidnapping case for Cassetti's escape from justice
deaths.notes: Twist: all twelve passengers are guilty; some wounds were post-mortem; Poirot presents two solutions and allows the passengers to go free

persons.name: Dr. Bartholomew Strange
persons.notes: Killed because he had begun to suspect Cartwright

persons.name: Hermione Lytton Gore
persons.notes: Cartwright's romantic obsession; her mother Lady Mary was intended to be a victim

persons.name: Reverend Babbington
persons.notes: Killed because he recognised Charles from years earlier when Charles used a different identity

persons.name: Sir Charles Cartwright
persons.notes: Famous actor; the killer

deaths.cause_detail: Nicotine introduced into his cocktail glass at a dinner party hosted by Sir Charles Cartwright; appeared to be sudden natural death
deaths.motive_detail: Babbington had known Cartwright years earlier under a different identity and was on the verge of recognising him

deaths.cause_detail: Nicotine in his cocktail at his own house party; staged to mirror Babbington's death and appear as a copycat or natural cluster
deaths.motive_detail: Strange had become suspicious of Cartwright

persons.name: Madame Giselle
persons.notes: Held financial secrets over many of the passengers

persons.name: Norman Gale
persons.notes: Used a blowpipe disguised among the wasp activity on the plane

deaths.cause_detail: Shot with a poisoned dart from a blowpipe while aboard an airliner over the English Channel; the dart was disguised as a wasp sting
deaths.motive_detail: Gale was indebted to Giselle and killed her to erase the debt and prevent exposure

persons.name: Alexander Cust
persons.notes: Epileptic travelling salesman; framed by Clarke to appear as the ABC killer

persons.name: Franklin Clarke
persons.notes: Killed his brother Sir Carmichael Clarke; created the ABC serial killer pattern as cover

persons.name: Sir Carmichael Clarke
persons.notes: Franklin's brother; the only intended victim

persons.name: Alice Ascher
persons.notes: First ABC victim; tobacconist in Andover.

persons.name: Betty Barnard
persons.notes: Second ABC victim; waitress in Bexhill.

persons.name: George Earlsfield
persons.notes: Third ABC victim; killed in Churston to obscure the real target.

deaths.cause_detail: Bludgeoned in her tobacco and newsagent shop in Andover; an ABC railway guide left open at Andover
deaths.motive_detail: Killed to establish the ABC serial pattern and distract from the true target (Sir Carmichael Clarke)

deaths.cause_detail: Strangled on Bexhill beach; ABC guide left open at Bexhill
deaths.motive_detail: Second killing to reinforce the ABC serial killer pattern

deaths.cause_detail: Bludgeoned at his estate in Churston; the true intended target of the entire scheme
deaths.motive_detail: Franklin stood to inherit his brother's considerable estate
deaths.notes: Twist: the apparent serial ABC murders were cover for a single inheritance murder

deaths.cause_detail: Stabbed at a cinema in Doncaster; wrong victim — Clarke intended to kill Donald Fraser who was investigating him
deaths.motive_detail: Continuing the ABC pattern; wrong victim was stabbed opportunistically

persons.name: Dr. Eric Leidner
persons.notes: Is actually Frederick Bosner, Louise's believed-dead first husband

persons.name: Louise Leidner
persons.notes: Had been receiving threatening letters; her first husband was Frederick Bosner

persons.name: Nurse Amy Leatheran
persons.notes: Narrator of the novel

deaths.cause_detail: Bludgeoned in her room using a rubber sandbag; the killer entered through the window using a specially constructed device
deaths.motive_detail: Louise was about to expose Dr. Leidner as her first husband Frederick Bosner, who had been a German spy and was believed executed
deaths.notes: Twist: the threatening letters Louise received were sent by her own husband, who was her supposedly dead first husband in disguise

persons.name: Anne Meredith
persons.notes: Had killed a previous employer by poisoning

persons.name: Dr. Roberts
persons.notes: The killer; had previously killed patients

persons.name: Mr. Shaitana
persons.notes: Hosted a dinner party where the four suspected murderers played bridge; stabbed during the game

persons.name: Mrs. Lorrimer
persons.notes: Confessed falsely to protect another; died by her own hand

deaths.cause_detail: Stabbed with a stiletto while seated in his armchair during a bridge game; the murder occurred in the same room as four other people, all playing cards
deaths.motive_detail: Shaitana had deduced Roberts was a murderer and was publicly exhibiting him as such

deaths.cause_detail: Poisoned (administered by Dr. Roberts who attended her as her physician) after she sent a false confession to police to protect someone else
deaths.motive_detail: Roberts poisoned Lorrimer to silence her and prevent her false confession from exposing him

persons.name: Jacqueline de Bellefort
persons.notes: Simon's true love; conspired with him to murder Linnet for her fortune

persons.name: Linnet Ridgeway Doyle
persons.notes: Married her best friend's fiancé Simon Doyle

persons.name: Louise Bourget
persons.notes: Witnessed the murder and attempted blackmail

persons.name: Simon Doyle
persons.notes: Shot Linnet while Jacqueline was establishing his alibi; also killed Louise the maid

deaths.cause_detail: Shot in the temple at close range while asleep in her cabin; Jacqueline shot Simon earlier that evening (a staged wound) to give him an alibi, after which Simon retrieved the gun and shot Linnet
deaths.motive_detail: Simon and Jacqueline planned to murder Linnet so Simon could inherit her fortune and reunite with Jacqueline
deaths.notes: Twist: Simon's leg wound was real but self-inflicted; his alibi (being with the doctor while Jacqueline appeared in the lounge) was carefully staged

deaths.cause_detail: Stabbed through the throat while lying in her bunk; she had signalled she knew the identity of Linnet's killer and was attempting blackmail
deaths.motive_detail: Louise witnessed events that implicated Simon and attempted to extort money

deaths.cause_detail: Shot by Jacqueline de Bellefort in their cabin after Poirot reveals the truth; a mercy killing and murder-suicide pact
deaths.motive_detail: Jacqueline killed Simon before turning the gun on herself; neither could face justice

deaths.cause_detail: Shot herself immediately after killing Simon Doyle

persons.name: Emily Arundell
persons.notes: Changed her will to leave everything to her companion Minnie Lawson shortly before dying

persons.name: Minnie Lawson
persons.notes: Poisoned Emily with phosphorus in her medicine; had befriended Emily to inherit her estate

deaths.cause_detail: Phosphorus introduced into Emily's liver pills by Minnie Lawson; caused jaundice and eventual death, appearing as natural liver failure
deaths.motive_detail: Lawson had manipulated Emily into changing her will and then poisoned her to inherit the estate
deaths.notes: Emily had written to Poirot before dying, suspecting one of her relatives; Poirot investigates after Emily is already dead

persons.name: Lady Westholme
persons.notes: Former American criminal (pickpocket Althea Boynton); killed to prevent exposure of her past

persons.name: Mrs. Boynton
persons.notes: Former prison wardress; psychologically abused her family for years

deaths.cause_detail: Injected with a fatal dose of digitoxin in her wrist while seated outside her tent in the ruins of Petra; her existing heart condition masked the murder
deaths.motive_detail: Mrs. Boynton recognised Lady Westholme from her criminal past in America and was about to expose her

persons.name: Alfred Lee
persons.notes: The loyal son who genuinely cared for his father

persons.name: Simeon Lee
persons.notes: Had an illegitimate son he didn't know; gathered his family for Christmas

persons.name: Superintendent Sugden
persons.notes: Illegitimate son of Simeon Lee; used his police role to control the investigation

deaths.cause_detail: Throat cut and body badly mutilated in his locked room on Christmas Eve; the locked-room puzzle is created by a trick with a key
deaths.motive_detail: Sugden is Simeon Lee's illegitimate son; Lee had seduced and abandoned Sugden's mother
deaths.notes: Twist: the investigating police officer is the killer and Lee's own illegitimate son

persons.name: Elinor Carlisle
persons.notes: Charged with murdering Mary Gerrard; Poirot proves her innocent

persons.name: Mary Gerrard
persons.notes: Niece of Elinor's aunt's lodgekeeper; stood to inherit from the aunt

persons.name: Nurse Hopkins
persons.notes: Killed Mary Gerrard using morphine stolen from her nursing bag

deaths.cause_detail: Morphine introduced into fish-paste sandwiches during a tea visit; Elinor Carlisle was present and was blamed, but the morphine came from Nurse Hopkins's stolen supply
deaths.motive_detail: Nurse Hopkins and Mary were related (Hopkins was her mother); Hopkins killed Mary to prevent her inheriting wealth that Hopkins believed she herself deserved
deaths.notes: Twist: the killer is Nurse Hopkins, not the accused Elinor Carlisle

persons.name: Alistair Blunt
persons.notes: Orchestrated the deaths to protect his identity and his conservative financial empire

persons.name: Amberiotis
persons.notes: Died from an anaesthetic overdose in Morley's dental chair

persons.name: Henry Morley
persons.notes: Poirot's own dentist; killed because he had recognised someone

deaths.cause_detail: Died in Henry Morley's dental chair from an overdose of local anaesthetic; initially appeared to be dental malpractice
deaths.motive_detail: Morley killed Amberiotis under instruction — or the death was arranged by others to silence a blackmailer
deaths.notes: The exact chain of responsibility is complex; Morley may have acted under duress

deaths.cause_detail: Found shot dead in his surgery; staged to appear as suicide
deaths.motive_detail: Morley had recognised Blunt from years earlier under a different identity

persons.name: Arlena Marshall
persons.notes: Flamboyant actress at a seaside resort; appeared to be having an affair with Patrick Redfern

persons.name: Christine Redfern
persons.notes: Patrick's wife; provided his alibi by impersonating Arlena while he killed her

persons.name: Patrick Redfern
persons.notes: Appeared infatuated with Arlena; actually conspiring with his wife to kill her

deaths.cause_detail: Strangled on a secluded beach cove; Patrick Redfern appeared to discover the body, but Christine had impersonated Arlena from a distance to give Patrick a false timeline
deaths.motive_detail: Redfern and Christine had conspired: Arlena had signed her assets over to Redfern and had to be killed before she could revoke this
deaths.notes: Twist: the apparent flirtation was an elaborate setup; Christine Redfern impersonated Arlena in a red bathing cap to create a false alibi

books.title: Five Little Pigs
books.creator: Agatha Christie
books.notes: Poirot investigates a 16-year-old case through witness memories alone

persons.name: Amyas Crale
persons.notes: Famous painter; was having an affair with his model Elsa Greer

persons.name: Angela Warren
persons.notes: Caroline's half-sister; accidentally introduced coniine into the beer

persons.name: Caroline Crale
persons.notes: Convicted of her husband's murder; died in prison; actually innocent — protected her half-sister

persons.name: Elsa Greer
persons.notes: Amyas's model and lover; one of the five suspects

deaths.cause_detail: Coniine introduced into his beer glass while he was painting; died while working at his easel
deaths.motive_detail: Angela, then a teenager, was furious at Amyas for a perceived injury; the poisoning was impulsive and she did not understand the full consequences
deaths.notes: Twist: Caroline was convicted but innocent; she took the blame to protect young Angela. The death is classified as manslaughter given Angela's age and impulsive state.

deaths.cause_detail: Died in prison of natural causes while serving a sentence for the murder of her husband
deaths.notes: Backstory death; occurs before the novel's main action

persons.name: Dr. John Christow
persons.notes: Brilliant doctor; having an affair with sculptor Henrietta Savernake

persons.name: Gerda Christow
persons.notes: John's devoted wife; shot him by the swimming pool after discovering his infidelity

persons.name: Henrietta Savernake
persons.notes: John's lover; tried to protect Gerda by throwing the gun into the pool

persons.name: Veronica Cray
persons.notes: John's former obsessive love interest; visited The Hollow and reignited tensions

deaths.cause_detail: Shot by the swimming pool at The Hollow; Gerda stood over him holding the gun when Poirot arrived; he gasped 'Henrietta' as he died
deaths.motive_detail: Gerda discovered John's affair with Henrietta and shot him in a moment of overwhelming jealousy
deaths.notes: Henrietta immediately threw the gun into the pool to protect Gerda; the apparent murderer is the actual killer

deaths.cause_detail: Took a fatal overdose of sleeping tablets after realising Poirot had identified her as the killer

persons.name: David Hunter
persons.notes: Rosaleen's brother; manipulative and protective of the inheritance

persons.name: Gordon Cloade
persons.notes: Killed in a wartime bombing; his new young wife inherited everything

persons.name: Rosaleen Cloade
persons.notes: Gordon's second wife who inherited his entire fortune; possibly a bigamist

persons.name: Rowley Cloade
persons.notes: Gordon's nephew; killed Enoch Arden to protect the Cloade family's finances

persons.name: Enoch Arden
persons.notes: Blackmailer posing under a false identity.

deaths.cause_detail: Killed in a wartime bombing raid in London; his unexpected death left his entire estate to his new wife Rosaleen, cutting off his extended family
deaths.notes: Backstory death; the inciting event for the novel's inheritance disputes

deaths.cause_detail: Bludgeoned at the local inn; Arden had claimed to know that Rosaleen's first husband Robert Underhay was still alive, making her marriage to Gordon bigamous and voiding the will
deaths.motive_detail: If Arden exposed Rosaleen's bigamy and Underhay were alive, the Cloade family would recover Gordon's fortune; Rowley killed Arden to silence him

deaths.cause_detail: Died from a drug overdose; circumstances suggest she was murdered by her own brother David Hunter
deaths.motive_detail: David needed to control the estate and feared Rosaleen would expose their secrets

persons.name: Evelyn Hope
persons.notes: Daughter of Eva Kane; killed Mrs. McGinty to conceal her mother's criminal past

persons.name: James Bentley
persons.notes: Convicted of Mrs. McGinty's murder; Poirot proves him innocent

persons.name: Mrs. McGinty
persons.notes: Killed because she recognised a photograph linking a local resident to an old murder case

deaths.cause_detail: Bludgeoned to death in her cottage; she had found a newspaper photograph linking a local person to the historical Craig murder case and showed it to her lodger James Bentley
deaths.motive_detail: McGinty had recognised that Evelyn Hope was the daughter of Eva Kane, a woman involved in the historical 'Craig' child murder case; exposure would have destroyed Evelyn's life

persons.name: Cora Lansquenet
persons.notes: Said at the funeral 'But he was murdered, wasn't he?' — killed the next day to silence her

persons.name: Miss Gilchrist
persons.notes: Cora's companion; killed both Cora and Richard to inherit; impersonated Cora after killing her

persons.name: Richard Abernethie
persons.notes: Died of gastroenteritis; actually poisoned

deaths.cause_detail: Poisoned with arsenic by Miss Gilchrist; death appeared to be from gastroenteritis
deaths.motive_detail: Gilchrist had been mentioned in Richard's will; she killed him to inherit and silenced anyone who might suspect
deaths.notes: Twist: Richard's death was initially believed natural; Cora's blurted remark at the funeral sets off the investigation

deaths.cause_detail: Hacked to death with a hatchet in her cottage the day after Richard's funeral; she had blurted out at the funeral that Richard had been murdered
deaths.motive_detail: Cora had unwittingly revealed she knew Richard was murdered; she had to be silenced

persons.name: Celia Austin
persons.notes: Had been stealing small items — compulsive behaviour linked to anxiety; murdered when she knew too much

persons.name: Nigel Chapman
persons.notes: Running a drug-smuggling operation out of the student hostel

persons.name: Patricia Lane
persons.notes: Killed after she became a threat to the smuggling operation

deaths.cause_detail: Killed with a morphine overdose staged as suicide; she had discovered the drug-smuggling operation and was becoming a threat
deaths.motive_detail: Celia had discovered Chapman's drug-smuggling activities hidden in student luggage

deaths.cause_detail: Struck on the head and killed; she had become suspicious of Nigel Chapman
deaths.motive_detail: Patricia was investigating the smuggling and getting too close to the truth

persons.name: Ariadne Oliver
persons.notes: Called Poirot because something about the murder-hunt 'felt wrong'

persons.name: Hattie Stubbs
persons.notes: Wife of Sir George Stubbs; disappeared during the fete; actually killed by her husband

persons.name: Marlene Tucker
persons.notes: Local girl cast as the 'victim' in a charity murder-hunt game; actually murdered

persons.name: Sir George Stubbs
persons.notes: Killed his wife Hattie and Marlene Tucker to stage events at the fete

deaths.cause_detail: Killed by her husband Sir George Stubbs before the fete and her body concealed
deaths.motive_detail: George had married Hattie for her money; she had to die before she could change her will
deaths.notes: Twist: the 'missing' Hattie had already been killed; her disappearance was staged to appear as a voluntary flight

deaths.cause_detail: Strangled in the boathouse while acting as the 'murder victim' in the fete's murder-hunt game
deaths.motive_detail: Marlene had seen something that could expose George's role in Hattie's disappearance

persons.name: Ann Shapland
persons.notes: Working for a criminal organisation seeking the Ramat jewels

persons.name: Hercule Poirot
persons.notes: Appears only in the final third of the novel

persons.name: Miss Bulstrode
persons.notes: Head of Meadowbank school; suspects something is wrong

persons.name: Miss Springer
persons.notes: Found the hidden jewels of Ramat in the sports equipment store

deaths.cause_detail: Shot in the sports equipment room at Meadowbank school; had discovered the valuable Ramat jewels hidden in a tennis racquet handle
deaths.motive_detail: Shapland was an agent sent to retrieve the Ramat royal jewels smuggled into the school; Springer had to be silenced

books.title: The Clocks
books.creator: Agatha Christie
books.notes: Poirot investigates from his armchair; unusual structure with a spy-thriller subplot

persons.name: Colin Lamb
persons.notes: Son of Superintendent Battle; reports to Poirot

persons.name: Hercule Poirot
persons.notes: Primarily investigates from his flat, consulting the case files

persons.name: Josefina Bland
persons.notes: Part of the spy network; killed to silence witnesses

persons.name: Ramsey
persons.notes: An unknown man found dead; connected to a Cold War espionage network

persons.name: Sheila Webb
persons.notes: Found the body of an unknown man in a blind woman's sitting room

deaths.cause_detail: Stabbed and found in the sitting room of blind Miss Pebmarsh surrounded by stopped clocks; the clocks were placed as a deliberate red herring
deaths.motive_detail: Ramsey had discovered the spy network operating in Crowdean and had to be eliminated
deaths.notes: The stopped clocks and blind woman's home are carefully staged red herrings

persons.name: Mary Restarick
persons.notes: Conspiring with her lover to gain Andrew Restarick's fortune; poisoning Norma to drive her to suicide or have her committed

persons.name: Norma Restarick
persons.notes: Believes she may have committed murder; actually being drugged and manipulated by her stepmother

deaths.cause_detail: Poisoned by his wife Mary and her accomplice
deaths.motive_detail: Mary and her lover planned to inherit Andrew's fortune

persons.name: Ariadne Oliver
persons.notes: Called Poirot after Joyce's murder

persons.name: Joyce Reynolds
persons.notes: Boasted at the party that she had once witnessed a murder; drowned minutes later

persons.name: Rowena Drake
persons.notes: Had killed her au pair years earlier; killed Joyce and Leopold to silence them

persons.name: Leopold Reynolds
persons.notes: Boy who claimed to have witnessed a murder; killed to silence him.

deaths.cause_detail: Head held down in the apple-bobbing tub at a Halloween party after she boasted of having witnessed a murder years before
deaths.motive_detail: Joyce had witnessed Rowena Drake murder her au pair girl years earlier; Drake killed her to prevent exposure

deaths.cause_detail: Drowned in a stream; Joyce's brother had also witnessed or knew something connected to the original murder
deaths.motive_detail: Leopold knew something that could have confirmed Joyce's original claim

persons.name: Ariadne Oliver
persons.notes: Drives the investigation by gathering witness memories

persons.name: General Ravenscroft
persons.notes: Died in an apparent double shooting with his wife on a clifftop

persons.name: Lady Ravenscroft
persons.notes: The truth: the woman who died was Lady Ravenscroft's mentally ill twin sister, not Lady Ravenscroft herself

deaths.cause_detail: Found shot dead on a clifftop alongside his wife in what appeared to be a mutual shooting or suicide pact; investigated retrospectively years later
deaths.motive_detail: Lady Ravenscroft killed her husband and then herself to end an impossible situation involving her mentally ill twin sister who had been impersonating her

deaths.cause_detail: Shot herself after killing her husband
deaths.notes: Twist: the woman who died in the clifftop incident was actually Lady Ravenscroft's twin sister (who had been living as Lady Ravenscroft due to the real Lady Ravenscroft's mental illness); the identities of the two women on the cliff are a central mystery

books.title: Curtain: Poirot's Last Case
books.creator: Agatha Christie
books.notes: Written in the 1940s but withheld for publication after Christie's death; Poirot's final case

persons.name: Arthur Hastings
persons.notes: Narrator; does not realise until the posthumous letter that Poirot killed Norton

persons.name: Barbara Franklin
persons.notes: Poisoned by her own husband after Norton manipulated him

persons.name: Hercule Poirot
persons.notes: Dies at the end of the novel; shoots Norton himself and then allows his heart to fail by stopping his medication

persons.name: Stephen Norton
persons.notes: Identified by Poirot as 'X': an instigator who psychologically manipulates susceptible people into committing murders

persons.name: Dr. Franklin
persons.notes: Researcher who unknowingly supplies the poison used to kill Barbara Franklin.

deaths.cause_detail: Poisoned with physostigmine in her coffee by her husband Dr. Franklin, who was manipulated into murder by Stephen Norton
deaths.motive_detail: Norton psychologically manipulated Franklin into believing his wife was an obstacle; Franklin acted at Norton's suggestion

deaths.cause_detail: Shot through the middle of his forehead while in bed; Poirot fired through the interconnecting wall between their rooms at Styles Court
deaths.motive_detail: Poirot determined that Norton was legally untouchable — he never committed crimes himself, only instigated them — and executed him to prevent future murders
deaths.notes: Twist: the killer is Poirot himself; revealed posthumously in a letter to Hastings

deaths.cause_detail: Died of heart failure after deliberately discontinuing his heart medication following Norton's execution; chose to die rather than live as an invalid who had committed murder
deaths.notes: Poirot's death — one of detective fiction's most famous endings

books.title: The Murder at the Vicarage
books.creator: Agatha Christie
books.notes: Miss Marple's debut novel

persons.name: Anne Protheroe
persons.notes: Killed her husband to be with her lover Lawrence Redding

persons.name: Colonel Protheroe
persons.notes: Disliked by almost everyone in St. Mary Mead; his death was welcomed by many

persons.name: Lawrence Redding
persons.notes: Anne's lover; confessed falsely to protect Anne

persons.name: Reverend Leonard Clement
persons.notes: Narrator of the novel; the vicar in whose study Protheroe was shot

deaths.cause_detail: Shot in the head in the vicar's study; both his wife Anne and her lover Lawrence Redding confessed, but the actual killer is Anne
deaths.motive_detail: Anne wanted to be free of her husband to be with Lawrence Redding
deaths.notes: Two false confessions complicated the investigation; Miss Marple deduced the truth from the forged time on a letter found at the scene

persons.name: Josie Turner
persons.notes: Ruby Keene's cousin; feared being supplanted; conspired with Mark Gaskell

persons.name: Mark Gaskell
persons.notes: Son-in-law of Conway Jefferson; conspired with Josie Turner to kill Ruby and Pamela

persons.name: Pamela Reeves
persons.notes: Killed and burned in a car to provide a false identity switch with Ruby Keene

persons.name: Ruby Keene
persons.notes: Found strangled in Colonel Bantry's library; was being cultivated by wealthy Conway Jefferson

deaths.cause_detail: Strangled and placed in Colonel Bantry's library to misdirect suspicion; Conway Jefferson intended to change his will in her favour
deaths.motive_detail: Conway Jefferson planned to adopt Ruby and leave her his fortune, cutting out his existing heirs Mark Gaskell and Adelaide Jefferson

deaths.cause_detail: Killed and her body burned beyond recognition in a car to serve as a false 'Ruby Keene' — allowing the killers to establish that Ruby had eloped and died in an accident
deaths.motive_detail: Pamela was killed to create a false second body that could be misidentified as Ruby's in a burned car
deaths.notes: Twist: the burned body in the car is not Ruby Keene but an innocent girl guide; Miss Marple identifies the switch through nail varnish and other details

persons.name: Agnes Woddell
persons.notes: Killed because she recognised the letter-writer

persons.name: Megan Hunter
persons.notes: Mrs. Symmington's stepdaughter; narrator's love interest

persons.name: Miss Marple
persons.notes: Appears only late in the novel

persons.name: Mr. Symmington
persons.notes: Wrote the poison-pen letters to cover the real murder of his wife; wanted to marry his children's governess Elsie Holland

persons.name: Mrs. Symmington
persons.notes: Received a poison-pen letter and apparently committed suicide; actually murdered

deaths.cause_detail: Took what appeared to be a suicide dose of cyanide after receiving an anonymous letter; actually murdered — her husband had sent the letter knowing she would take it as proof of an affair and die rather than face the shame
deaths.motive_detail: Symmington wanted to marry his children's governess Elsie Holland; he wrote the poison-pen letters to cover this specific murder
deaths.notes: Twist: the poison-pen letters were written by the solicitor himself — not an anonymous malicious villager — specifically to murder his wife under cover of a general harassment campaign

deaths.cause_detail: Strangled in the Symmington house; she had recognised the handwriting on the letters and confronted the killer
deaths.motive_detail: Agnes knew who had written the poison-pen letters

persons.name: Dora Bunner
persons.notes: Charlotte's old schoolfriend; accidentally began to reveal Charlotte's true identity

persons.name: Letitia Blacklock
persons.notes: Actually Charlotte Blacklock, impersonating her dead sister Letitia to inherit Randall Goedler's fortune

persons.name: Rudi Scherz
persons.notes: Hired to burst in with a gun and create a diversion; shot dead by the real killer

deaths.cause_detail: Shot dead in the dark during a staged 'announcement' at Little Paddocks; Scherz was meant to fire blanks and flee, but was shot by the real killer using a second gun
deaths.motive_detail: Scherz knew too much about the killer's identity and plans; the staged event gave an opportunity to kill him and the actual target (Miss Blacklock) at once

deaths.cause_detail: Her aspirin tablets replaced with a fatal drug; she was on the verge of unwittingly revealing Charlotte's true identity
deaths.motive_detail: Bunner's memories of the real Letitia Blacklock were beginning to emerge in her conversation; she had to be silenced

persons.name: Carrie Louise Serrocold
persons.notes: Miss Marple's old friend; her charitable rehabilitation institution is the setting

persons.name: Christian Gulbrandsen
persons.notes: Discovered that money was being embezzled from the institution

persons.name: Lewis Serrocold
persons.notes: Carrie Louise's husband; the embezzler and killer

deaths.cause_detail: Shot during the carefully staged distraction of a young delinquent's attack on Lewis Serrocold; the disturbance was manufactured to give Serrocold an alibi
deaths.motive_detail: Gulbrandsen had discovered that Serrocold was embezzling the charitable trust's funds and had to be stopped before he could expose him
deaths.notes: Twist: the entire violent incident with the young offender was staged as cover for the murder

persons.name: Adele Fortescue
persons.notes: Rex's young second wife; poisoned while having tea (the queen eating bread and honey)

persons.name: Gladys Martin
persons.notes: Strangled and found with a clothes peg on her nose (the maid hanging out the clothes)

persons.name: Miss Marple
persons.notes: Comes to investigate because of Gladys Martin, a former maid she trained

persons.name: Percival Fortescue
persons.notes: Rex's elder son; poisoned his father with taxine from yew trees to inherit

persons.name: Rex Fortescue
persons.notes: Died at his city office; rye grain found in his pocket (the blackbirds baked in a pie)

deaths.cause_detail: Taxine introduced into his marmalade at breakfast; died at his city office; rye was found sewn into his coat pocket
deaths.motive_detail: Percival wanted to inherit the Fortescue fortune and take full control of the family business
deaths.notes: The nursery rhyme 'Sing a Song of Sixpence' structure runs through all three murders

deaths.cause_detail: Cyanide introduced into her afternoon tea; found dead in the drawing room
deaths.motive_detail: Adele as widow would have taken a large portion of the estate; Percival eliminated her

deaths.cause_detail: Strangled in the garden with a clothes peg placed on her nose; she had witnessed something that could identify the killer
deaths.motive_detail: Gladys had seen or knew something incriminating about Percival

books.title: 4:50 from Paddington
books.creator: Agatha Christie
books.notes: Also published as 'What Mrs. McGillicuddy Saw!'

persons.name: Alfred Crackenthorpe
persons.notes: Killed Martine and Edmund to protect the estate from being divided

persons.name: Edmund Crackenthorpe
persons.notes: Son of Luther Crackenthorpe; poisoned

persons.name: Martine Crackenthorpe
persons.notes: Her body is eventually found in a sarcophagus at Rutherford Hall

persons.name: Miss Marple
persons.notes: Sends her friend Lucy Eyelesbarrow to investigate undercover

persons.name: Mrs. McGillicuddy
persons.notes: Saw a woman being strangled in a passing train; the only witness

deaths.cause_detail: Strangled on a train and her body dumped at Rutherford Hall; Mrs. McGillicuddy witnessed the murder through the train window
deaths.motive_detail: Martine was the widow of the eldest Crackenthorpe son Edmund; she and her son would have inherited a large share of the Crackenthorpe estate

deaths.cause_detail: Poisoned at the family meal
deaths.motive_detail: Edmund was suspicious of Alfred; Alfred poisoned him to remove a rival heir and potential witness

books.title: The Mirror Crack'd from Side to Side
books.creator: Agatha Christie

persons.name: Heather Badcock
persons.notes: Eagerly told Marina how she had gone to meet her years ago despite having German measles, which infected the pregnant Marina and caused her baby's disability

persons.name: Jason Rudd
persons.notes: Marina's husband; suspected the truth but covered for his wife

persons.name: Marina Gregg
persons.notes: Killed Heather Badcock — the woman whose innocent action caused Marina's baby to be born with congenital rubella

deaths.cause_detail: Marina Gregg had poisoned her own cocktail intending to kill herself; Heather Badcock picked up Marina's glass by mistake and drank the poisoned cocktail
deaths.motive_detail: Marina recognised Heather as the woman who, by visiting her while ill with German measles, caused Marina's unborn child to be born severely disabled
deaths.notes: Twist: the intended victim was not Heather Badcock but Marina herself — Heather died by accidentally taking Marina's poisoned drink

deaths.cause_detail: Died of a drug overdose; by the end of the novel she has been identified as the killer

persons.name: Jason Rafiel
persons.notes: Disabled millionaire who helps Miss Marple

persons.name: Lucky Dyson
persons.notes: Has murdered two previous husbands; conspiring with Edward Hillingdon

persons.name: Major Palgrave
persons.notes: Killed before he could show Miss Marple a photograph of a known murderer

persons.name: Molly Kendal
persons.notes: Runs the Golden Palm hotel with her husband Tim

persons.name: Victoria Johnson
persons.notes: Maid at the hotel who saw too much and was killed to silence her.

deaths.cause_detail: His usual heart medication was replaced with an overdose; died in his sleep; he had been about to show Miss Marple a photograph of a murderer
deaths.motive_detail: Palgrave was about to identify Lucky's photograph as that of a known wife-murderer

deaths.cause_detail: A local worker who had seen something relevant to Major Palgrave's death; killed to prevent her speaking
deaths.motive_detail: Victoria had witnessed something that could link the killers to Major Palgrave's death

persons.name: Bess Sedgwick
persons.notes: Famous for daredevil exploits; the brains behind the gang using Bertram's Hotel as a criminal base

persons.name: Elvira Blake
persons.notes: Bess Sedgwick's secret daughter; targeted for murder by an accomplice

persons.name: Michael Gorman
persons.notes: Actually Bess Sedgwick's former husband; knew too much about the criminal operation

deaths.cause_detail: Shot in the street outside Bertram's Hotel; was Bess Sedgwick's long-estranged husband who knew the truth about the criminal organisation based at the hotel
deaths.motive_detail: Gorman threatened to expose the criminal gang using Bertram's Hotel for robbery logistics

persons.name: Clothilde Bradbury-Scott
persons.notes: Killed multiple young women out of obsessive jealousy; had hidden one victim's body in her garden for years

persons.name: Jason Rafiel
persons.notes: Died before the novel begins; left Miss Marple a legacy in return for solving 'a crime'

persons.name: Miss Marple
persons.notes: Commissioned posthumously by Jason Rafiel to investigate an unspecified crime

persons.name: Nora Broad
persons.notes: One of several girls murdered by the serial killer

persons.name: Verity Hunt
persons.notes: Young woman strangled years before the main plot; the murder Miss Marple investigates.

deaths.cause_detail: Killed by Clothilde Bradbury-Scott out of obsessive possessive jealousy; body hidden in the garden of The Old Manor House for years
deaths.motive_detail: Clothilde was obsessively attached to Verity and killed her when Verity fell in love with Michael Rafiel
deaths.notes: Twist: an innocent man (Michael Rafiel, Jason's son) was convicted of a different murder; the real crime was Verity Hunt's buried in the garden

deaths.cause_detail: Strangled and her body substituted for Verity Hunt's in order to frame Michael Rafiel for Verity's murder
deaths.motive_detail: Nora's body was used to fake Verity's murder in a public place, sending investigators in the wrong direction

books.title: Sleeping Murder
books.creator: Agatha Christie
books.notes: Written in the 1940s; Miss Marple's final case, published posthumously

persons.name: Dr. James Kennedy
persons.notes: Helen's brother; obsessively controlling; killed Helen when she tried to leave her husband and build her own life

persons.name: Gwenda Reed
persons.notes: As a small child witnessed her stepmother's murder; bought the same house 18 years later, triggering repressed memories

persons.name: Helen Halliday
persons.notes: Gwenda's stepmother; strangled 18 years before the main action; Gwenda witnessed it as a child

deaths.cause_detail: Strangled 18 years before the main action by her brother Dr. Kennedy; small Gwenda witnessed the murder but repressed the memory
deaths.motive_detail: Kennedy had an obsessive, possessive relationship with his sister Helen; he killed her rather than allow her to leave her husband and live independently
deaths.notes: Backstory murder; the entire novel builds towards uncovering this long-buried crime

persons.name: Jane Finn
persons.notes: Sole survivor of the Lusitania disaster to have the secret documents; had amnesia

persons.name: Mr. Brown
persons.notes: The mysterious master criminal; true identity concealed until the end

persons.name: Rita Vandemeyer
persons.notes: Associate of Mr. Brown poisoned before she could talk.

deaths.cause_detail: Poisoned by Mr. Brown to prevent her from revealing his identity to Tommy and Tuppence
deaths.motive_detail: Vandemeyer was about to expose Mr. Brown's true identity

books.title: N or M?
books.creator: Agatha Christie
books.notes: Second World War spy thriller; Tommy and Tuppence hunt Nazi agents in a seaside boarding house

persons.name: Carl von Deinim
persons.notes: Suspected as a German agent; innocent

persons.name: Mrs. Sprot
persons.notes: Revealed to be a German spy; uses her own child as a shield

persons.name: Tuppence Beresford
persons.notes: Independently infiltrates the boarding house

persons.name: Sheila Perenna
persons.notes: Shot by Mrs. Sprot to prevent exposure of the spy ring.

deaths.cause_detail: Shot by enemy agents after being exposed as knowing too much about the spy network at Sans Souci
deaths.motive_detail: Sheila had stumbled upon evidence of the spy ring

books.title: By the Pricking of My Thumbs
books.creator: Agatha Christie

persons.name: Ada Fanshawe
persons.notes: Tommy's aunt; hinted before her death that something was wrong at the nursing home

persons.name: Emma Boscowan
persons.notes: Responsible for a series of child murders; lived near the canal cottage

persons.name: Mrs. Lancaster
persons.notes: Spoke cryptically about 'a child behind a fireplace'; disappeared from the nursing home

persons.name: Tuppence Beresford
persons.notes: The main investigator in this novel

persons.name: child victims
persons.notes: Multiple unnamed children killed by Emma Boscowan over many years.

deaths.cause_detail: Died at the nursing home under suspicious circumstances; she had been in good health but her medication was tampered with
deaths.motive_detail: Ada had seen or heard something about the children's murders and had to be silenced

deaths.cause_detail: Multiple children were killed by Emma Boscowan and their bodies hidden near her cottage; discovered during the investigation
deaths.motive_detail: Pathological motive for child murder; details not fully elaborated in the narrative

books.title: Postern of Fate
books.creator: Agatha Christie
books.notes: Christie's final novel; Tommy and Tuppence's last case

persons.name: KK
persons.notes: The long-concealed traitor responsible for Mary Jordan's death; identity uncovered at the end

persons.name: Mary Jordan
persons.notes: Killed decades ago while investigating a treasonous spy ring; her death was hidden in a children's book code

deaths.cause_detail: Killed decades before the main action; her death was hidden and her murder encoded in a children's book found in the Beresfords' new home
deaths.motive_detail: Mary Jordan was investigating a spy ring and was killed to protect the traitor's identity
deaths.notes: Backstory murder that drives the entire investigation

books.title: And Then There Were None
books.creator: Agatha Christie
books.notes: Best-selling crime novel of all time; originally published as 'Ten Little Niggers'

persons.name: Emily Brent
persons.notes: Dismissed her servant for being pregnant; the servant killed herself

persons.name: General MacArthur
persons.notes: Sent his wife's lover to certain death in WWI

persons.name: Justice Wargrave
persons.notes: The murderer; faked his own death and killed all ten guests; acted as self-appointed executioner

persons.name: Philip Lombard
persons.notes: Abandoned 21 native men in Africa to die

persons.name: Vera Claythorne
persons.notes: Let her charge Cyril Hamilton drown to clear her lover Hugo's inheritance

persons.name: Anthony Marston
persons.notes: First victim; poisoned cyanide in his drink.

persons.name: Mrs. Rogers
persons.notes: Second victim; overdosed with chloral hydrate in her sleep.

persons.name: Thomas Rogers
persons.notes: Third victim; killed with a chopper while chopping wood.

persons.name: Dr. Armstrong
persons.notes: Drowned by Wargrave after being manipulated into complicity.

persons.name: William Blore
persons.notes: Crushed by a marble clock bear pushed from a window.

deaths.cause_detail: Potassium cyanide introduced into his whisky glass at dinner on the first evening
deaths.motive_detail: Wargrave appointed himself executioner of those who had killed but escaped justice; Marston had run down two children

deaths.cause_detail: Given an overdose of sleeping medication; did not wake up
deaths.motive_detail: Mrs. Rogers and her husband had let their former employer die without calling a doctor to inherit her money

deaths.cause_detail: Struck from behind while gazing at the sea; had said he did not expect to leave the island
deaths.motive_detail: MacArthur had sent his wife's lover to certain death in WWI

deaths.cause_detail: Found dead with an axe wound to the head while chopping wood
deaths.motive_detail: Rogers and his wife let their employer die without summoning medical help

deaths.cause_detail: Injected with potassium cyanide while sitting in the kitchen; found with a bee on the table (the rhyme: 'a bumblebee stung one')
deaths.motive_detail: Emily Brent's rigidity drove her pregnant maid to suicide

deaths.cause_detail: Appeared to be shot dead in his chair; the wound was theatrical — he faked his death using a special squib and a red herring
deaths.notes: Twist: Wargrave faked his own death; he actually died last by a self-inflicted shot after killing all others

deaths.cause_detail: Thrown off the cliff into the sea by Wargrave after Armstrong had been lured into helping Wargrave fake his death
deaths.motive_detail: Armstrong had performed an operation while drunk, causing a patient's death

deaths.cause_detail: A heavy marble clock pushed from an upstairs window crushed his skull
deaths.motive_detail: Blore had given false evidence that sent an innocent man to prison, where he died

deaths.cause_detail: Shot by Vera Claythorne on the beach; she had retrieved his revolver believing him to be the killer
deaths.motive_detail: Vera believed Lombard was the last remaining killer and shot him in perceived self-defense
deaths.notes: Vera's act was technically a mistake — Lombard was not Wargrave

deaths.cause_detail: Hanged herself by the noose that Wargrave had arranged in her room; the sight of it triggered a compulsion — she walked into it almost without will
deaths.notes: Wargrave had set up the noose knowing Vera's guilt would drive her to use it; functionally a murder by manipulation

deaths.cause_detail: Shot himself in the head after writing a confession in a manuscript thrown into the sea in a bottle; died as the last and final victim of his own game
deaths.notes: The real final death; Wargrave's 'apparent' murder earlier was staged. His confession reveals the entire truth.

books.title: The Man in the Brown Suit
books.creator: Agatha Christie
books.notes: Early standalone adventure thriller; features Colonel Race

persons.name: Anne Beddingfeld
persons.notes: Narrator; stumbles into an international criminal conspiracy

persons.name: Nadina
persons.notes: A woman with jewels and secrets; killed at the Mill House

persons.name: The Colonel
persons.notes: The 'Colonel' is the main villain — identity revealed as Harry Rayburn's father

persons.name: unknown man at tube station
persons.notes: Unidentified man who falls onto the tracks; his death triggers the plot.

persons.name: unknown
persons.notes: Killer identity unknown at the time of the tube station death.

deaths.cause_detail: Fell or was pushed onto the London Underground track at Hyde Park Corner station; Anne Beddingfeld finds a cipher in his coat pocket
deaths.notes: Inciting incident; whether this was accident, suicide, or murder is part of the mystery

deaths.cause_detail: Strangled at the Mill House by the Colonel to prevent her from selling stolen diamonds and exposing the criminal organisation
deaths.motive_detail: Nadina possessed evidence that could expose the Colonel's criminal network

books.title: Murder is Easy
books.creator: Agatha Christie
books.notes: Also published as 'Easy to Kill'

persons.name: Gordon Easterfield
persons.notes: Honoria's obsession; the indirect cause of all the murders

persons.name: Honoria Waynflete
persons.notes: The serial killer of Wychwood; killed multiple villagers out of obsessive passion for Gordon Easterfield

persons.name: Luke Fitzwilliam
persons.notes: Investigates after meeting an old woman on a train

persons.name: Miss Lavinia Fullerton
persons.notes: Told Luke she knew someone was committing serial murders in her village; killed before she could report to Scotland Yard

persons.name: Tommy Pierce
persons.notes: Nasty young man; pushed from a height by Honoria Waynflete.

persons.name: Dr. Humbleby
persons.notes: Local doctor poisoned to prevent him exposing the killer.

deaths.cause_detail: Struck down on her way to London to report the murders to Scotland Yard; her death appeared to be a traffic accident
deaths.motive_detail: Fullerton had identified Honoria as the serial killer and was about to expose her
deaths.notes: The murder that sets off Luke's investigation

deaths.cause_detail: A village boy pushed from a height; one of several suspicious deaths in Wychwood
deaths.motive_detail: The boy had seen something he should not have
deaths.notes: One of multiple village murders

deaths.cause_detail: Died of septicaemia from a wound that was secretly infected by the killer
deaths.motive_detail: Dr. Humbleby was inconvenient to Honoria's scheme

books.title: Death Comes as the End
books.creator: Agatha Christie
books.notes: Set in ancient Egypt circa 2000 BC; Christie's only historical mystery novel

persons.name: Hori
persons.notes: The family's trusted scribe; helps Renisenb uncover the truth

persons.name: Nofret
persons.notes: Young concubine brought home by Imhotep; her presence sets off a chain of murders

persons.name: Renisenb
persons.notes: Narrator figure; returns home to her father's house after her husband dies

persons.name: Yahmose
persons.notes: One of Imhotep's sons; revealed as the killer of multiple family members

persons.name: Satipy
persons.notes: Wife of Yahmose; pushed to her death after threatening to expose him.

persons.name: Sobek
persons.notes: Son of Imhotep; poisoned by Yahmose.

deaths.cause_detail: Fell to her death from a cliff path; initially ruled accidental but Imhotep believes the family killed her
deaths.motive_detail: Nofret was turning Imhotep against his family and manipulating his will; the family acted to remove her
deaths.notes: Her death triggers the subsequent murders

deaths.cause_detail: Fell from the same cliff path shortly after Nofret
deaths.motive_detail: Satipy suspected the truth about Nofret's death

deaths.cause_detail: Poisoned wine at a family gathering
deaths.motive_detail: Sobek was becoming suspicious of Yahmose

books.title: Towards Zero
books.creator: Agatha Christie
books.notes: Features Superintendent Battle; the premise is that all events converge on a single 'zero hour' murder

persons.name: Audrey Strange
persons.notes: Nevile's ex-wife; framed for the murder

persons.name: Lady Tressilian
persons.notes: Killed in her bed; golf club used as the weapon

persons.name: Nevile Strange
persons.notes: Planned the murder meticulously; arranged for suspicion to fall on others

deaths.cause_detail: Beaten to death with a golf club (niblick) in her bedroom at night; Nevile Strange had carefully constructed a frame-up pointing to his ex-wife Audrey
deaths.motive_detail: Lady Tressilian was about to change her will and remove Nevile; he had also planned to reunite with Audrey and needed to eliminate obstacles

books.title: Crooked House
books.creator: Agatha Christie
books.notes: Christie's own favourite among her books; no series detective

persons.name: Aristide Leonides
persons.notes: Self-made millionaire patriarch; insulin replaced with eserine

persons.name: Brenda Leonides
persons.notes: Aristide's much younger second wife; suspected along with her young lover Laurence Brown

persons.name: Charles Hayward
persons.notes: Narrator; his lover Sophia's grandfather is murdered

persons.name: Josephine Leonides
persons.notes: The killer; a highly intelligent child who killed her grandfather and Nanny Saunders

persons.name: Nanny Saunders
persons.notes: Killed by Josephine to silence her

persons.name: Edith de Haviland
persons.notes: Kills Josephine Leonides by running her over, then takes her own life.

deaths.cause_detail: His insulin was replaced with eserine (physostigmine sulphate), causing death from the injected dose; appeared as an accidental insulin overdose
deaths.motive_detail: Josephine felt overlooked and not special; killed from a mixture of excitement at committing a 'perfect crime' and resentment at not being the centre of attention
deaths.notes: One of Christie's most shocking twists: the killer is an 11-year-old girl

deaths.cause_detail: Arsenic introduced into her cocoa; Nanny had begun to suspect the truth about Aristide's murder
deaths.motive_detail: Nanny Saunders was on the verge of realising that Josephine had killed her grandfather

deaths.cause_detail: Died in a car accident when her grandmother Edith de Haviland drove her off the road; Edith chose this end to prevent Josephine from standing trial
deaths.motive_detail: Edith, the family's moral centre, chose to end Josephine's life rather than see the child tried for murder and ruin the family
deaths.notes: Edith de Haviland also died in the car crash; a private execution and suicide combined

books.title: Ordeal by Innocence
books.creator: Agatha Christie
books.notes: Christie called this one of her personal favourites; no series detective

persons.name: Arthur Calgary
persons.notes: Could have provided an alibi for Jacko Argyle but was away on expedition; returns to clear his name

persons.name: Hester Argyle
persons.notes: The actual killer; could not bear her adoptive mother's suffocating dominance

persons.name: Jacko Argyle
persons.notes: Convicted of Rachel's murder and died in prison; posthumously cleared

persons.name: Rachel Argyle
persons.notes: Adoptive mother of five children; murdered by one of them

deaths.cause_detail: Struck over the head with a poker in her study
deaths.motive_detail: Hester could not tolerate Rachel's controlling, suffocating hold over the family; killed her in a desperate act of rebellion
deaths.notes: Twist: Jacko was convicted and died for a crime he did not commit; Calgary's return triggers the real killer's exposure

deaths.cause_detail: Died in prison while serving his sentence for a murder he did not commit
deaths.notes: Backstory; a miscarriage of justice that haunts the novel

books.title: The Pale Horse
books.creator: Agatha Christie
books.notes: Features Ariadne Oliver; the thallium poisoning is now famous for real-world forensic cases it inspired

persons.name: Ariadne Oliver
persons.notes: Christie's self-parody character

persons.name: Thyrzta Grey
persons.notes: Runs The Pale Horse as an occult cover for a murder-for-hire scheme

persons.name: Zachariah Osborne
persons.notes: The real killer behind the 'witchcraft' operation; supplies thallium to a murder-for-hire network

persons.name: Father Gorman
persons.notes: Priest killed by Osborne after hearing a dying confession implicating the scheme.

persons.name: multiple unnamed victims
persons.notes: Victims poisoned via thallium as part of a paid murder-for-hire scheme.

deaths.cause_detail: Struck on the head in the street after hearing a dying woman's confession that contained the names of murder targets
deaths.motive_detail: Gorman had written down the names of the targets; the network needed those names destroyed

deaths.cause_detail: Multiple people across England died from thallium poisoning, administered by Osborne on behalf of paying clients through the Pale Horse network
deaths.motive_detail: Osborne ran a murder-for-hire business; clients paid to have relatives or enemies killed
deaths.notes: Twist: the occult practitioners at The Pale Horse are a deliberate red herring; the actual killing mechanism is thallium poisoning by the pharmacist Osborne

books.title: Endless Night
books.creator: Agatha Christie
books.notes: Stylistically different from Christie's other work; written as a psychological thriller with an unreliable narrator

persons.name: Ellie Rogers
persons.notes: Rich American heiress who married Michael; he married her to get her fortune, then had her killed

persons.name: Greta Andersen
persons.notes: Michael's true lover; conspired with him to kill Ellie

persons.name: Michael Rogers
persons.notes: The narrator and killer; in love with Gipsy's Acre and conspired to inherit the land and fortune

deaths.cause_detail: Killed through an induced allergic reaction by Greta Andersen; Michael and Greta conspired to kill Ellie so Michael could inherit her fortune and be free to live with Greta at Gipsy's Acre
deaths.motive_detail: Michael and Greta planned to kill Ellie so Michael would inherit; they had planned this even before Michael's marriage to Ellie
deaths.notes: Twist: the narrator Michael is revealed in the final pages to have been the architect of his wife's murder from the start; the apparent love story is exposed as premeditated murder

deaths.cause_detail: Killed by Michael after she began to become a liability
deaths.motive_detail: Greta knew everything and had become a danger to Michael

books.title: The Secret of Chimneys
books.creator: Agatha Christie
books.notes: First appearance of Superintendent Battle. Anthony Cade is revealed to be Prince Nicholas of Herzoslovakia.

persons.name: Anthony Cade
