deaths.notes: Rollins planted a bomb in the governor's car at Dale the Whale's direction; Monk intercepted the trigger signal and saved the governor's life. Rollins was arrested and Monk's name was cleared.

tv_episodes.title: Mr. Monk Buys a House
tv_episodes.creator: Andy Breckman
tv_episodes.notes: Monk buys the house of recently deceased Joseph Moody to escape a noisy neighbor. The death was no accident — a hidden cache of 1968 bank-robbery money is behind two murders.

deaths.notes: Cassie Drake, Moody's private nurse, pushed him down the stairs to keep the hidden $4 million a secret.
Jake stabbed his girlfriend/accomplice Cassie after Monk exposed her role and she became a liability.

tv_episodes.title: Mr. Monk and the Genius
tv_episodes.creator: Andy Breckman
tv_episodes.notes: Chess grandmaster Patrick Kloster poisons his wife Linda for her $10 million insurance policy, mirroring an earlier crime against his first wife Tatiana.

deaths.notes: Patrick used oleander-derived poison to cause a fatal heart attack, killing Linda for her $10 million insurance policy.

tv_episodes.title: Mr. Monk Gets Lotto Fever
tv_episodes.creator: Andy Breckman
tv_episodes.notes: Natalie becomes the California lottery announcer after the previous one is murdered; the lottery machine had been rigged.

deaths.notes: Billy stabbed lotto announcer Marissa with scissors to prevent her from exposing the rigged lottery machine.

tv_episodes.title: Mr. Monk Takes a Punch
tv_episodes.creator: Andy Breckman
tv_episodes.notes: Monk tries to protect boxer Ray Regis from an assassin hired because Regis knows about stolen paintings. A bomb in a punching bag kills an innocent bystander.

deaths.notes: Hitman Charles Bach planted a bomb in Ray Regis's punching bag; vagrant Eddie accidentally triggered it.

tv_episodes.title: Mr. Monk Is Underwater
tv_episodes.creator: Andy Breckman
tv_episodes.notes: Monk investigates the death of a submarine executive officer aboard the USS Seattle. The killer staged a suicide to conceal a career-ending navigation error.

deaths.notes: Whitaker forced Pierce to drink vodka at gunpoint then shot him and staged it as a suicide to prevent Pierce from revealing a cover-up of a submarine accident.

tv_episodes.title: Mr. Monk Falls in Love
tv_episodes.creator: Andy Breckman
tv_episodes.notes: A war criminal working as a cab driver is murdered by a refugee's mother; Monk insists the beautiful suspect Leyla is innocent.

deaths.notes: Leyla's mother stabbed war criminal Karsten Emerik (the Butcher of Zemenia) with a hat pin in the back of his taxi cab.

tv_episodes.title: Mr. Monk's 100th Case
tv_episodes.creator: Andy Breckman
tv_episodes.notes: Monk's 100th case is covered by a TV news magazine. While watching the broadcast, Monk realizes a second, separate killer exploited the serial killer's MO to commit an unrelated murder.

deaths.motive_detail: Serial killer Douglas Thurman strangled multiple young actresses
deaths.notes: Serial killer Douglas Thurman strangled multiple young actresses; Cassandre Rank was one of his victims.
TV journalist James Novak strangled his mistress Kate Kindel and mimicked Thurman's MO to frame the serial killer.

tv_episodes.title: Mr. Monk Gets Hypnotized
tv_episodes.creator: Andy Breckman
tv_episodes.notes: Sally Larkin fakes her own kidnapping to frame her husband for it, then murders him to avoid a prenup and claim his estate.

deaths.notes: Sally killed her real-estate mogul husband Aaron to collect his fortune before their divorce finalized, staging a fake kidnapping as a diversion.

tv_episodes.title: Mr. Monk and the Miracle
tv_episodes.creator: Andy Breckman
tv_episodes.notes: A pharmacist murders a homeless man who witnessed him painting fake miracle messages, and hides his dead partner's body on monastery grounds to prevent a renovation.

deaths.notes: Pharmacist Owen McCloskey suffocated homeless man Willie T., who had seen him painting fake miracle messages, then placed the body in a junkyard refrigerator.

tv_episodes.title: Mr. Monk's Other Brother
tv_episodes.creator: Andy Breckman
tv_episodes.notes: Monk's escaped half-brother Jack Jr. is accused of murdering a prison social worker, but the real killer is the corrupt guard who set him up as a patsy.

deaths.notes: Prison guard Daniel Reese murdered social worker Lindsey Bishop (his affair partner) and framed Jack Jr. for the killing to cover up the relationship.

tv_episodes.title: Mr. Monk on Wheels
tv_episodes.creator: Andy Breckman
tv_episodes.notes: A corporate assistant steals genetically modified seeds by having a bicycle thief copy the lock combination; when the thief draws too much attention she kills him.

deaths.notes: Sarah Longson, Dr. Berry's assistant, shot bicycle thief Kuramoto in a cemetery after he drew too much police attention to her seed-theft scheme.

tv_episodes.title: Mr. Monk and the Lady Next Door
tv_episodes.creator: Andy Breckman
tv_episodes.notes: Monk befriends a sweet elderly neighbor while investigating a Guinness museum robbery and a subsequent jewelry store murder by the same thief.

deaths.notes: John Keyes killed Guinness museum security guard David Elliot during a robbery of an egg-eating robot exhibit.

tv_episodes.title: Mr. Monk Makes the Playoffs
tv_episodes.creator: Andy Breckman
tv_episodes.notes: A backup quarterback is bludgeoned to death by his own driver after catching the driver selling the team's playbook to the opposition.

deaths.notes: Limo driver Shawn Metzger bludgeoned quarterback David Gitelson with a tire iron when Gitelson threatened to report him for selling the team's playbook.

tv_episodes.title: Mr. Monk and the Bully
tv_episodes.creator: Andy Breckman
tv_episodes.notes: Monk's childhood bully hires him to follow his wife; the case uncovers a scheme in which an evil twin kills her own partner to take all the money.

deaths.notes: Patrice Gesner stabbed her partner Douglas Fendle with a steak knife to avoid sharing the proceeds of their scheme.

tv_episodes.title: Mr. Monk and the Magician
tv_episodes.creator: Andy Breckman
tv_episodes.notes: Monk's neighbor Kevin Dorfman is killed while filling in for a magician who uses his act to smuggle drugs and kills to protect the secret.

deaths.notes: Magician Karl Torini strangled Kevin Dorfman with a piano wire after Kevin discovered that Torini's stage equipment was used to smuggle drugs.

tv_episodes.title: Mr. Monk Fights City Hall
tv_episodes.creator: Andy Breckman
tv_episodes.notes: Monk chains himself to the garage where Trudy died to prevent demolition. Councilwoman Eileen Hill, who agreed to halt the demolition, is murdered by a man desperate to keep his affair secret.

deaths.notes: Paul Crawford strangled city councilwoman Eileen Hill with a custom necktie to prevent her from exposing their affair and threatening his wealthy marriage.

tv_episodes.title: Mr. Monk's Favorite Show
tv_episodes.creator: Andy Breckman
tv_episodes.notes: Monk idolizes the star of his childhood favorite TV show, but she is the murderer — she killed the accountant who had helped her rig an awards show and later tried to blackmail her.

deaths.notes: Actress Christine Rapp killed accountant Victor Timlinson after he tried to blackmail her over their conspiracy to rig a Silver Globe award.

tv_episodes.title: Mr. Monk and the Foreign Man
tv_episodes.creator: Andy Breckman
tv_episodes.notes: Monk bonds with Nigerian widower Samuel Waingaya over their shared grief and investigates his wife's hit-and-run death, which was concealed by murdering a witness.

deaths.notes: Drunk driver Kenneth Nichols struck and killed Ansara Waingaya in a hit-and-run while distracted on the phone.
Nichols beat housekeeper Maria Fuentes with a crowbar because she had heard him on the phone during the hit-and-run and could identify him.

tv_episodes.title: Mr. Monk and the UFO
tv_episodes.creator: Andy Breckman
tv_episodes.notes: Monk's car breaks down in a Nevada town where a fake UFO sighting draws internet enthusiasts — and a body. The killer staged the UFO story to lure people to find his sister's corpse so he could claim her inheritance.

deaths.notes: Kyle Larkin killed his sister Marge to inherit the family fortune, dumping her body in the desert where it went undiscovered until he staged a UFO hoax to attract searchers.

tv_episodes.title: Mr. Monk Is Someone Else
tv_episodes.creator: Andy Breckman
tv_episodes.notes: Monk goes undercover as deceased hit man Frankie DePalma to foil a mob assassination plot; the real target was never the nominal victim but someone else entirely.

deaths.notes: Mob boss Jimmy Barlowe had Stanley Greenblatt killed to flush out Greenblatt's son Alvin, who had skimmed money from one of Jimmy's casinos.

tv_episodes.title: Mr. Monk Takes the Stand
tv_episodes.creator: Andy Breckman
tv_episodes.notes: Sculptor Evan Gildea murders his estranged wife and is acquitted after his lawyer shreds Monk on the stand, causing Monk to doubt himself.

deaths.notes: Sculptor Evan Gildea bludgeoned his estranged wife Nancy with a marble sculpture; he used one that lacked his initials to avoid damaging his own work.

tv_episodes.title: Mr. Monk and the Critic
tv_episodes.creator: Andy Breckman
tv_episodes.notes: Theater critic John Hannigan murders his mistress to protect his career and wealthy engagement, while Natalie tries to prove he wasn't even watching Julie's performance.

deaths.notes: Theater critic John Hannigan pushed waitress Callie Esterhaus over a hotel balcony to prevent her from exposing their affair and ruining his prestigious engagement.

tv_episodes.title: Mr. Monk and the Voodoo Curse
tv_episodes.creator: Andy Breckman
tv_episodes.notes: Voodoo dolls seem to predict deaths; only one is an actual murder. A paramedic exploited real accidents to create the illusion of a curse and poisoned her uncle for his inheritance.

deaths.notes: Paramedic Angeline Dilworth poisoned her uncle Robert Boyd with an untraceable drug to mimic a heart attack and claim his cell phone company fortune.

tv_episodes.title: Mr. Monk Goes to Group Therapy
tv_episodes.creator: Andy Breckman
tv_episodes.notes: Monk joins group therapy when his insurance runs out; someone starts killing off his fellow patients who could identify him as Tiffany Bolt's murderer.

deaths.notes: Xavier Danko murdered exotic dancer Tiffany Bolt out of obsession, then began killing therapy group members who could place him near the scene.
Xavier killed group therapy patient Barbara O'Keefe in her pool to prevent her from identifying him as Tiffany's killer.

tv_episodes.title: Happy Birthday, Mr. Monk
tv_episodes.creator: Andy Breckman
tv_episodes.notes: On Monk's 50th birthday, a janitor is killed after taking his vacuum cleaner invention to a patent attorney who then double-crosses his own partner.

deaths.notes: Patent attorney Meckler murdered janitor Bradley Foster, the real inventor of a self-cleaning vacuum, to steal credit for the invention.
Pressman poisoned his partner Meckler with tainted ice cubes to avoid splitting the vacuum cleaner profits.

tv_episodes.title: Mr. Monk and Sharona
tv_episodes.creator: Andy Breckman
tv_episodes.notes: Sharona returns to collect a settlement after her uncle's death at a country club; Monk determines the fall was no accident but a murder arranged by the victim's golf partner.

deaths.notes: Golfer Perry Walsh lured Howie into a staged fall that turned into a real murder when Walsh pushed him to conceal their affair.

tv_episodes.title: Mr. Monk and the Dog
tv_episodes.creator: Andy Breckman
tv_episodes.notes: Monk cares for a missing woman's dog; when the woman's body is found, the dog's puppies become crucial evidence linking the killer to the victim.

deaths.notes: Steven DeWitt shoved his mistress Amanda Castle during an argument about hiding evidence of his marriage; her head struck the kitchen counter and she died.

tv_episodes.title: Mr. Monk Goes Camping
tv_episodes.creator: Andy Breckman
tv_episodes.notes: Monk joins a police camping trip; armed robbers who killed an armored car guard stalk the group to recover an incriminating bullet casing one of the boys picked up.

deaths.notes: Brothers Del and Luke Johnston shot an armored car guard during a robbery; a stray bullet casing found by a camping boy tied the brothers to the crime.

tv_episodes.title: Mr. Monk Is the Best Man
tv_episodes.creator: Andy Breckman
tv_episodes.notes: Stottlemeyer prepares to marry T.K. Jensen; his eco-terrorist ex-fiancee kills a blackmailing former accomplice and plants a gun in the captain's tuxedo to derail the wedding.

deaths.notes: Former eco-terrorist Stephanie Briggs shot her ex-accomplice Martin Kettering when he tried to blackmail her about their shared past.

tv_episodes.title: Mr. Monk and the Badge
tv_episodes.creator: Andy Breckman
tv_episodes.notes: Monk is reinstated to the SFPD and discovers a fellow officer was killed not by the serial killer everyone suspects but by a greedy tipster who wanted the full reward.

deaths.notes: Window-washer Manny Alvarez killed Officer DiMarco to collect the full serial killer reward himself rather than splitting it as agreed.

tv_episodes.title: Mr. Monk and the End (Part 1)
tv_episodes.creator: Andy Breckman
tv_episodes.notes: Series finale Part 1: Monk is poisoned and near death. Trudy left him a video message revealing her affair with Judge Rickover and the child she bore. Monk also uncovers that Rickover murdered nurse Wendy Stroud to bury the secret.

deaths.notes: Judge Ethan Rickover planted a car bomb that killed Trudy Monk twelve years before the series finale, to prevent her from connecting him to nurse Wendy Stroud's disappearance.
Rickover murdered the nurse who delivered his secret child with Trudy and buried her body in his front yard to keep the affair hidden.

tv_episodes.title: Mr. Monk and the End (Part 2)
tv_episodes.creator: Andy Breckman
tv_episodes.notes: Series finale Part 2: Monk survives, confronts Judge Rickover who confesses then shoots himself. Monk discovers Trudy's daughter Molly Evans survived and was adopted.

deaths.notes: The series finale resolves the twelve-year mystery of Trudy Monk's car bombing; Judge Rickover is confirmed as the killer before taking his own life.
Rickover grabbed Monk's gun and shot himself after his guilt was exposed and his career was ruined.

persons.name: Beth Chadwick
persons.notes: Shot her controlling brother Bryce to gain her freedom. Claimed it was an accident (mistook him for a burglar) but Columbo proves it was premeditated.

persons.name: Bryce Chadwick
persons.notes: Beth's domineering older brother whose controlling nature cost him his life.

persons.name: Elliot Markham
persons.notes: Architect who murders his wealthy client and hides the body in a building's concrete foundation.

persons.name: Beau Williamson
persons.notes: Wealthy client whose body was concealed in concrete at a construction site.

persons.name: Jarvis Goodland
persons.notes: Tony's uncle who orchestrated a fake kidnapping scheme to access trust fund money, then murdered Tony to eliminate him.

persons.name: Tanner
persons.notes: Character identity uncertain; originally entered as victim in error.

persons.name: Sir Roger Haversham
persons.notes: Wealthy English theatrical producer whose death the killers attempt to conceal.

persons.name: Nicholas Frame
persons.notes: Veteran Shakespearean stage actor; husband of Lillian Stanhope. Conspires with his wife to conceal the death of their producer.

persons.name: Lillian Stanhope
persons.notes: Veteran Shakespearean stage actress; wife of Nicholas Frame. Conspires with her husband to conceal the death of their producer.

tv_episodes.title: Any Old Port in a Storm
tv_episodes.creator: Richard Levinson, William Link

tv_episodes.title: The Bye-Bye Sky High I.Q. Murder Case
tv_episodes.creator: Richard Levinson, William Link

tv_episodes.title: Caution: Murder Can Be Hazardous to Your Health
tv_episodes.creator: Richard Levinson, William Link

tv_episodes.title: Columbo and the Murder of a Rock Star
tv_episodes.creator: Richard Levinson, William Link

deaths.notes: Killed as a decoy to make the murder of Gregory Tannin look like a Red John killing.
Melanie was bound and gagged; the killers accidentally smothered her while trying to silence her during an abduction. The stab wound found on the body was noted as not fatal.

deaths.motive_detail: Danny struck the guard during a confrontation over trespassing; the death resulted from the guard falling and hitting his head on heavy equipment.
deaths.notes: Security guard who confronted the group at the construction site; Danny punched him and he fell, cracking his head on heavy equipment.

deaths.motive_detail: The group killed Christine to prevent her from reporting the accidental death of the security guard. Hope also acted out of jealousy.
deaths.notes: Hope struck Christine with a shovel; Danny then dragged her to a water trough and drowned her. All four friends were arrested.

deaths.cause_detail: Bled to death from torture wounds (pliers and lit cigars) after locking himself in his panic room vault
Blunt force trauma to the head; body hidden in a freezer.
deaths.motive_detail: Daniel killed Meier after being confronted about using his wife as collateral for gambling debts.
Clara killed her mother out of rage after Rosemary disinherited Travis and refused to listen to her children's concerns.
Partners killed Dave Martin to seize full ownership of the valuable Aldensgrove aquifer.
Tommy avenged Dave Martin's murder by killing the business partners responsible.
Tommy attempted to kill Piller as revenge for Dave Martin's murder.
Stern poisoned Nelson to prevent him from exposing Lewis Stutzer's neuroscience research as fraudulent and protecting the university's reputation.
Stern staged Kerry's death as a suicide to eliminate her as a liability after she became a risk of exposing his involvement.
deaths.notes: Death staged to look like a suicide, with a brief note saying 'forgive me' and a bottle of poison left at the scene.
Mrs. Renfrew killed Undine after discovering she was her husband's illegitimate daughter and Jared's half-sister, then framed Jared for the crime.
Killed by Red John in Tijuana before he could reveal information about Red John's identity. Left a cryptic dying message: "He is ma...".
Killed by Red John alongside Jared Renfrew in Tijuana, apparently as a witness.
Died in prison; had claimed at his 1998 murder trial that Red John was the real killer and he was only an accomplice.
The intermediary who paid Vanna Clooney to lie at Jared Renfrew's trial was later killed by a bus.

tv_episodes.title: Red Rum
tv_episodes.creator: Andi Bushell
tv_episodes.notes: Season 1, Episode 12. Directed by Dean White. Inspired by the murder of Christopher DeNoyer.

deaths.motive_detail: Michael killed Cody during an argument after Cody confronted him about abusing Brad; Michael staged the scene as a ritual killing to conceal his involvement and protect his reputation.
Haightly secretly loved Katie and believed Fricke had hurt her; he targeted Fricke to protect her, but shot Claire by mistake.
Haightly intended to kill Fricke, believing he had hurt Katie whom Haightly secretly loved.
Heather blamed Scarlett for her daughter Rachel's fatal car accident, caused by pills Rachel obtained that originated from Scarlett's drug dealing.
Patience intended to continue Scarlett's pill-dealing operation, so Heather killed her to finish what she had started and prevent the drug business from carrying on.

persons.name: Dan Hollenbeck
persons.notes: Real name Daniel Kraeger, son of Paul Kraeger

deaths.motive_detail: Dan Hollenbeck blamed Medina for firing his father Paul Kraeger, which contributed to his father's downfall.
Lisbon shot Dan to prevent him from killing Patrick Jane and Grace Van Pelt.
Whittaker's death was staged as a random killing to disguise Faulk's true target, Nadia Sobell.
Sobell was preparing a sexual harassment suit against Faulk, planning to defect to a competitor, and shorting Carnelian's stock.
Lindsay was obsessed with Dr. Royston Daniel and wanted to remove her sister Mary Beth, who was romantically involved with him, from the picture.
deaths.notes: Lindsay drove from San Francisco under cover of a trip alibi, killed her sister, then hypnotized Rick Tiegler to believe he had committed the murder and hypnotized Carl Resnik to deliver the body to the police.

deaths.motive_detail: Felicia felt Felix was suffocating her by threatening to pull funding over script changes; she manipulated Brandon Fulton into luring and shooting Felix.
Felicia lured Brandon to her home under false pretenses and shot him to silence him and stage it as self-defense.

deaths.notes: Knox survived the attempt; she left a note saying 'I'm sorry'.
Killed by Red John before the episode begins; found in a state park with Red John's signature markings.
Jane shoots Hardy to prevent him from killing Lisbon.
Body was staged to look like a suicide by jumping off a bridge by a hired fixer.
The fixer died in a car accident outside CBI Headquarters while fleeing.

deaths.motive_detail: Milton Howard hired a shooter to kill McTier and frame Lisbon to discredit her testimony against him.

deaths.notes: Foster was thrown from a second-floor window of the Beckworth mansion, mimicking the 1928 death of original owner Walter Beckworth. The crime was staged to appear as a haunting.

deaths.motive_detail: Sandrine Gerber killed Kirby to prevent him from revealing their affair to her husband and his wife.

deaths.notes: Kidnap victim found as a desiccated corpse in an abandoned building, dead 3-5 years. Believed killed by the same group who kidnapped Mia Westlake.
Van Pelt was shot three times in the chest and torso; she survived because she was wearing a Kevlar vest.
Shot twice in the back by Rigsby as she was about to ambush Hicks. Identified post-mortem as Colin Haymer's former girlfriend.
Body discovered during a building renovation; killed to prevent identification of Red John; body subsequently stolen by Rebecca Anderson.
Shot in the abdomen at CBI headquarters; died in hospital.
Shot during the jewel heist at the charity event; described as lingering near death.
Found shot in his hotel room closet; Tom Doverton arrested for this murder at the episode's conclusion.
Reporter killed victim during a confrontation over a career-defining story she had withheld from him; necklace taken as a souvenir.

deaths.motive_detail: The killer poisoned the victim after learning he had sabotaged her liquor license application, ending her dream of opening her own restaurant.
The killer also targeted this victim for using her connection to block the killer's liquor license application.
Welks had been stealing small amounts of the virus to sell on the black market; he exposed Alicia to cover for the missing quantity so she would take the fall.

deaths.notes: Camille survived the shooting but suffered amnesia; she is the Jane Doe found by the agents.
One of two hikers who stumbled upon Willis removing IDs from bodies at the lake and were shot at close range.
One of two hikers who stumbled upon Willis removing IDs from bodies at the lake and were shot at close range.

deaths.motive_detail: Sylvan was hired by ADA Marc Odenthal to kill Kelly Flower so Odenthal could advance to District Attorney by taking over her high-profile case.
Odenthal shot Sylvan to silence him after Sylvan's court case was thrown out, preventing him from revealing who hired him.
Resentment over Noah's emotional coldness, lack of income, and refusal to accept a $2 million prize for his code-breaking device.
deaths.notes: Killer disguised as a clown and placed a clown convention ad as a smokescreen. Victim's severed finger was used to open his fingerprint-locked safe.
Copycat Red John murder committed by Dylan and Ruth for a real-murder slasher film.
The real Red John kills the TV interviewer who interviewed Kristina Frye.
Red John shoots Dylan during confrontation in the abandoned building.
Red John slices Ruth's throat after shooting her, killing her despite her pleas.

deaths.cause_detail: Raped and had her throat cut by Henry Dahl years before the episode's events.
deaths.notes: Backstory death; occurred years prior to episode events.

deaths.cause_detail: Struck in the head with what appears to be a liquor bottle. Max Winter subsequently shot the already-dead body in the face multiple times.
deaths.notes: Max Winter also shot the corpse in the face but Dahl was already dead; Sean Meyers is the actual killer.
Overdose on drugs provided by Dr. Saban to manage her addiction; her body was concealed by Saban.
Head of security killed in office explosion intended for another target.
Rachel shoots her own accomplice in the basement where Jane is held captive.
Hightower shoots Rachel to save Jane and Lisbon from being burned alive.

deaths.motive_detail: Lailah Bloom killed patients out of a deep-seated grudge against alcoholics, stemming from her parents' drinking problems and alcohol-related deaths.

deaths.notes: Died from contaminated soda sold by Esther and Nilda; his death prompted Valerie's investigation which led to the killings.
Killed before the episode's events; the cold case is the central mystery. The killer manipulated security footage to frame Ron Crosswhite.
Documentary filmmaker killed because she was investigating the circumstances of Ginger Crosswhite's death and getting too close to the truth.
Paralegal grazed in the shoulder by a sniper shot from Officer Rowley that missed its intended target. Non-fatal wound.
Antiques dealer found stabbed at a museum; framed evidence points to Hightower.

deaths.cause_detail: Shot in the stomach and throat slit
Consumed a lethal substance in tea, dying peacefully by assisted suicide witnessed by Patrick Jane

deaths.motive_detail: Erica wanted to keep expanding the matchmaking business while John sought to retire; she used an accomplice (her assistant Peter) to establish a false alibi.
Kieran killed the first-chair violinist and framed the second so that Ariel, whom he was obsessively infatuated with, could advance to concert master.

deaths.notes: Dinkler's roommate, shot execution style; collateral damage by the bomber's handler.
Found dead approximately four days earlier; shot in the knee (torture) and head.
Went over the hotel balcony edge to the pavement below rather than be captured; circumstances of fall ambiguous — treated as homicide/unclear but listed with no identified killer.
One of the two Sheriff's office deputies shot by O'Laughlin outside the gate of Hightower's safe house.
One of the two Sheriff's office deputies shot by O'Laughlin outside the gate of Hightower's safe house.
Red John's mole; shot by Grace and Hightower while distracted.
Shot in self-defense by LaRoche after picking his way out of cuffs and reaching for a gun in the police car.
Shot in the stomach by Jane at the mall; Jane's long-awaited confrontation.
Dinkler's roommate, killed execution-style as collateral damage.
Found dead at home with gunshot wounds to the knee and head; tortured before being killed.
The assassin went over a balcony edge to the pavement below rather than be captured; death_type uncertain as it may be suicide or accidental.
One of two Sheriff's deputies shot and killed by Craig O'Laughlin outside the gate.
One of two Sheriff's deputies shot and killed by Craig O'Laughlin outside the gate.
Lisbon was shot by O'Laughlin but survived; she was wounded at Hightower's safe house.
Red John's mole, revealed to be Craig O'Laughlin; shot by Grace and Hightower while he was distracted.
Shot in self-defense by LaRoche after the attendant picked his way out of cuffs and reached for a gun.
The season finale's central act: Jane shoots the man he believes to be Red John in a shopping mall cafe.
Suicide by sharpened spoon; left a note about Red John.
One of two prior child victims attributed to the serial killer dubbed 'The Balloon Man' before the episode's events.
Second prior child victim attributed to 'The Balloon Man' before the episode's events.

persons.name: Henry Tibbs
persons.notes: Fits the profile of a mass shooter; harboured plans to shoot the Mayor

persons.name: Willie Shubert
persons.notes: Falsely confessed to the murder of Scott Gibson

deaths.notes: Gibson was found dead in an alley during an immigration rally. The killer was identified from photos on Gibson's memory card — an unnamed homeless man caught on camera approaching Gibson with a knife.
Tibbs opened fire at a crowd in a park including the CBI team; no one was injured as the crowd were undercover agents. Cho arrested Tibbs for attempted murder.

persons.name: William Gardner
persons.notes: Real name Eddie Fish; changed his appearance after murdering Talia's father

deaths.cause_detail: Crushed by a blow or heavy pressure; also sexually assaulted
deaths.motive_detail: Killed to prevent her from exposing him as her father's murderer

deaths.cause_detail: Stabbed 18 times
deaths.motive_detail: Drug addict who fled with a large amount of money and changed his appearance
deaths.notes: Past murder that triggered the central investigation; William Gardner is the alias of Eddie Fish

tv_episodes.title: Where in the World is Carmine O'Brien?
tv_episodes.creator: David Appelbaum

deaths.motive_detail: Chad killed the chief to prevent her from exposing his drug dealing operation.
Chad killed Steve with the same gun used on the chief, to prevent him from witnessing or revealing the drug dealing.

deaths.cause_detail: Bound, throat slit, dumped with objects placed over eyes; first victim of the San Joaquin killer
Escalated killing; eyes removed rather than covered with objects
Found in an alley beneath Red John's trademark smiley face; killed after publicly insulting Red John on TV

deaths.motive_detail: Bianca Trotter was a corrupt officer who had made a deal with drug dealer Omar Vega. She killed Yoli because Yoli had become suspicious of her.
deaths.notes: Yoli was shot from behind, two to the head, outside the nightclub while leaving after receiving an upsetting text message.

deaths.motive_detail: Bianca killed the Perry Boys to frame them for Yoli's murder and to steal their drugs.
deaths.notes: One of the Perry Boys; killed the same night as Yoli and with the same gun. Found dead at their house.

deaths.motive_detail: Bianca killed the Perry Boys to frame them for Yoli's murder and to steal their drugs.
deaths.notes: One of the Perry Boys; was holding a video game controller when killed. Found dead at their house alongside the other Perry Boy.

deaths.motive_detail: Steve Penn was jealous of Doc Dugan's fame and wanted Doc dead so his collection of signed memorabilia would increase in value; Jake was an unintended victim who happened to be in Doc's SUV.
deaths.notes: Jake was Doc Dugan's assistant. He was in Doc's SUV as a decoy while Doc was hiding to avoid a subpoena. The bomb was intended for Doc.

deaths.motive_detail: Steve Penn was jealous of Doc's fame and stood to profit financially from his death through the increased value of his Doc Dugan memorabilia collection.
deaths.notes: Doc Dugan was the intended target of the car bomb but was not present when it detonated.

deaths.motive_detail: Satterfield recognized Tom's mask from the bank robbery and had to be silenced.
Guards killed during a bank robbery to eliminate witnesses.
Guards killed during a bank robbery to eliminate witnesses.

deaths.notes: Father of Panzer's first victim; kills himself.

deaths.motive_detail: Nett orchestrated the killing of the eldest Porchetto son to take over the family crime business, as the youngest son was considered useless.
Van Pelt shot the hitman who had been sent to kill her and Janpen in the woods.
Amy Barron killed Ruiz and framed Eve Mulberry in order to remove her business partner and take control of the vineyard to bring in outside investors.
deaths.notes: Amy used a tool from the vineyard bearing Eve's fingerprints as the murder weapon, and wore a blond wig to impersonate Eve.

deaths.motive_detail: During a fight when he picked her up on the night of the murder, he strangled her to death.
Meadows threatened to expose Hill as his informant to cult leader Stiles; Hill killed him to prevent this.

Kati's in-laws murdered her to prevent a divorce that would cause the family financial loss. They drugged wine she was bringing to a picnic, waited until she and James Barca were unconscious, then stabbed her and planted the knife in Barca's hand to frame him.

deaths.cause_detail: Smothered to death.
deaths.motive_detail: Gary Philo, Cruz's boss, killed her. He was her secret Shakespeare chat partner and silenced her to conceal the relationship or information she had on him.

Firth had been taking bribes to place patients in drug trials; she negligently placed Braddock without accounting for his alcohol-damaged liver, which caused the trial drug to accelerate his death rather than treat his cancer. She then stole and burned his body to prevent an autopsy from uncovering her scheme.
Trish killed Ryan because she could not trust him after he showed no remorse for killing Shelly.
Ryan killed Shelly to silence her as a witness to Holly's kidnapping.
McTerny witnessed Ms. Austin and Billy together and threatened to report them to the principal.

tv_episodes.title: So Long, and Thanks for All the Red Snapper
tv_episodes.creator: Ashley Gable

deaths.motive_detail: Dispute over dividing sunken ship treasure; Lance wanted a larger share and killed Jay in a rage during a scuffle.
Benjamin Marx killed Antonio Castro to prevent him from exposing Marx's embezzlement from the firm.
deaths.notes: Antonio was lured away from a fundraiser under the pretense of a hazing ritual and locked alive in a storage cabinet in an abandoned warehouse; his body was found after approximately six months.
Unidentified man witnessed by Kevin Cintron and Manfreddo shooting himself in the face with a shotgun. Suicide.

Found dead and duct-taped in the back of the limo in place of Red John. Red John arranged for Wainwright to be in the limo, and Agent Darcy's FBI gunfire killed him unknowingly — Red John orchestrated this as a trap and demonstration of his reach.

tv_episodes.title: Ruby Slippers
tv_episodes.creator: Daniel Cerone
tv_episodes.notes: Season 4, Episode 21. The apparent murder is revealed to be a staged fake death orchestrated by a drag cabaret group to protect the intended victim from further abuse.

persons.name: Archie Bloom Jr.
persons.notes: Staged his own death with the help of the cabaret group to escape abuse.

persons.name: Glennda Snow
persons.notes: Lead performer at drag cabaret; works at a funeral home; helped stage the fake death.

deaths.notes: Archie attempted to burn himself alive in his car (handcuffed to the steering wheel) to escape years of abuse, but was talked out of it by the cabaret group. The group then staged his death using a stolen unidentified body to give him a new life.
Rex attacked Callie first; she struck him with a champagne bottle in self-defence.
Yannick was the responding police officer who found Callie unconscious and killed her to steal her winning lottery ticket.

deaths.motive_detail: Betty wanted to steal Victor's diamonds; she laced his tea with belladonna to induce hallucinations, then manipulated him into killing himself with a saw.
Nancy discovered Ernie planned to flee to Brazil alone after she had helped him transform his life; she killed him out of betrayal and jealousy.
Moss killed Hoff to provoke a gang war between the Overtons and Low Riders, allowing his own gang to take over both criminal territories.

deaths.cause_detail: Steve was shot by Moss and had a shotgun pellet lodged near his heart that could not be safely removed; he subsequently suffered a fatal stroke.
deaths.motive_detail: Moss shot Steve (who was trying to help Hoff) as part of his scheme to incite a gang war. Steve later died from a stroke while recovering from the gunshot wound.

deaths.notes: Moss was shot by Rigsby after Moss attempted to shoot Rigsby following a feigned inability to move.

deaths.motive_detail: Kim shot Dellinger to prevent him from revealing a corrupt deal in which Kim had hidden evidence of Dellinger's earlier vehicular manslaughter in exchange for a favorable court ruling on a bar lawsuit.

deaths.cause_detail: Dellinger struck Mia with his car while driving drunk; charges were dropped to a simple DUI after Kim concealed evidence.

deaths.notes: Security guard shot while trying to intervene in the kidnapping; killing was unplanned according to the kidnapper.
Sloan ordered her accomplice to kill Gary during the kidnapping; Gary was rescued before the order was carried out.

Car brakes were tampered with by Ed Hunt (acting on Volker's instigation) causing Cassie's car to go off a cliff; she survived the crash but was then suffocated by an unnamed Volker associate at the scene.
Staged as a suicide (hanging) with a fake email suicide note. Volker watched as his associate carried out the killing.
An unnamed hunter shot during a company retreat at Carrington woods; the shooters mistook him for a deer. The death preceded the episode and was covered up by Dilmer, Phipps, and Bosch.

deaths.motive_detail: Lemuel was beaten to death to prevent him from going to the police about the accidental shooting of a hunter during the Carrington retreat.
deaths.notes: Beaten to death at the Catalina 2 model home. Autopsy showed he was struck with several different blunt objects.

deaths.motive_detail: Gold killed Jeremy to obtain his valuable marijuana seeds and profit from their sale.
Lyle killed Charlie after convincing her to retrieve the stolen ruby necklace, to conceal her own theft driven by compulsive gambling debts.
Charles Milk was killed in a drive-by shooting orchestrated by Tommy Volker to silence him before investigators could reach him.
Jones was a geologist about to testify against Tommy Volker; he was killed to prevent his testimony.
deaths.notes: Death occurred two months before the episode's main timeline (shown in flashback). Volker ordered the killing; Milk carried it out.

deaths.motive_detail: Clyde stepped in front of a bus after Volker threatened his life, knowing he was a dead man either way.
deaths.notes: Suicide: Clyde killed himself by stepping in front of a speeding bus after receiving a death threat from Volker.

deaths.motive_detail: Volker attempted to kill Marvin to eliminate him as a witness to the Horatio Jones murder. Lisbon shot Volker before he could fire.
deaths.notes: Attempted murder thwarted by Lisbon shooting Volker in the shoulder at the zoo.
Cold case from circa 1988. Holly shot Bradovich with a shotgun to defend her mother, Dr. Ellen Preston, after he attacked her.
Cold case from circa 1988. Body found in the barn basement alongside Bradovich and Talbot. Killer unknown; Holly denied involvement. Possibly connected to Red John or Ray Haffner.
Cold case from circa 1988. Body found in the barn basement alongside Bradovich and Charney. Killer unknown; Holly denied involvement. Possibly connected to Red John or Ray Haffner.

deaths.motive_detail: Professor Friedman killed Linda to claim her discovery of the California jade moth as his own, concealing her academic contribution.
deaths.notes: Linda's body was found as a skeleton inside a tank of flesh-eating insects used for specimen preparation. A scientific instrument found in the tank was identified as a possible murder weapon, but the exact cause of death was not confirmed in the narrative.

deaths.motive_detail: Elise killed Lissie to use her body as a stand-in for her own, faking her death to escape legal and financial troubles.

deaths.notes: Lorelei tortured and killed Julia Howard, believing she was involved in her sister Miranda's death.
Lorelei shot a security guard who confronted her during her attempt to abduct one of the shelter trustees.
Lorelei shot Lennon after he confessed to facilitating her sister Miranda's death. He survived and was taken by ambulance; Kirkland's Homeland Security team then took custody of him.
Lorelei was found dead two weeks after the main events, bearing Red John's trademark smiley face in blood. Red John killed her to silence her before she could reveal his identity to Jane.

deaths.cause_detail: Throat slit
deaths.motive_detail: Lucy had discovered discrepancies in the drug logs and wanted to mount a full investigation; the doctor killed her to conceal his prescription fraud scheme
Sharon discovered Dodge disguised as the fictitious investor Burt Hanover; he killed her to keep the fraud secret.

deaths.cause_detail: Air embolus caused by syringe of air injected into IV line.
deaths.motive_detail: Kirkland injected air into Lennon's IV while he was coming out of a coma, creating a fatal air embolus, to prevent Lennon from revealing information connected to Red John.
Ian killed Hollis during an argument after Hollis revealed that Lily was his daughter — Ian was having an affair with Lily and sought to prevent the revelation from becoming public.
Elizabeth went to Missy's house to scare her; when Missy called Buddy a creep, Elizabeth shot her in a fit of rage.

deaths.notes: Locksmith found dead at LaRoche's house during a break-in to steal a tupperware container. Cause of death not explicitly stated.
Backstory death: LaRoche's mother committed suicide three months after being sexually assaulted. Her death is the emotional core of the episode's mystery surrounding the tupperware container.
Found dead in a motel room; Red John's trademark smiley face left at the scene. Specific cause of death not stated in the source.
Killed by Red John for revealing that Jane shook Red John's hand; revealed via DVD recording found in an envelope delivered by Miriam Gottlieb.
Woman found dead at the opening crime scene; her fiancé Cody Benbow is quickly deduced by Jane to be the killer.
Man found buried in the desert at Salton Sea, missing for two years; three diner employees killed him for money.
Forensics tech found near death in a condemned house; whispers 'Tyger, Tyger' to Lisbon before dying. Killed by Red John, narrowing the suspect list to six.
Lisbon is attacked by Red John in the condemned house after finding Brett Partridge; left with status unknown at episode end, a Red John smiley drawn in blood on her face.

deaths.motive_detail: Stone staged his own death via drone strike to escape the consequences of deceiving the Pentagon about the Redbird drone project.
Red John killed Sophie Miller because she could identify him from their therapy sessions.

deaths.notes: Larry Kincaid, a pizza delivery man, was found dead in a duck pond in Napa Valley. He was in town for his niece's wedding.
Charlie took Patrick Jane hostage on a church roof while attempting to steal a valuable Bible. Sheriff McAllister shot Charlie to rescue Jane.
Marx was on Jane's fake list of Red John suspects. His body is discovered at the start of the episode; he was killed by Kirkland's associates who stole the list.
Kirkland is shot by Reede Smith during a staged prison transport escape. Smith staged it to look like the driver shot Kirkland while he was fleeing.

deaths.motive_detail: Parkman shot Frost to prevent him from exposing their past relationship and the fact that it began when Frost was a minor.

Frost stabbed Parkman during the confrontation in which Parkman was shooting him. The stab nicked Parkman's aorta; the wound proved fatal when Parkman later strained to launch the gun out of his hotel window with an exercise band.
Tinsley was attacked (fatally wounded) by Red John or Red John's agent, apparently because she had learned something about Red John's identity.

deaths.notes: A man found hanging from a noose over the edge in a parking garage; discovered by Reede Smith and an FBI agent. Identity and circumstances not established in this episode.

Bertram first bashed the bartender over the head with a scotch bottle, then stabbed him repeatedly with the broken bottle after the bartender recognised him from a TV news report identifying him as a serial killer.

persons.name: Thomas McAllister
persons.notes: Sheriff of Napa County; true identity of serial killer Red John.

persons.name: Gale Bertram
persons.notes: CBI director; member of the Blake Association, used as a scapegoat by Red John.

persons.name: Oscar Cordero
persons.notes: Henchman of the Blake Association acting on McAllister's orders.

deaths.notes: Bertram, a Blake Association member, arranged to meet Jane at an Alexandria Cemetery church claiming he would reveal Red John's identity. McAllister (the real Red John) then appeared and ordered Oscar Cordero to shoot Bertram, framing the scenario so Bertram would be found dead alongside Jane and believed to be Red John.

After a foot chase ending near a creek, Jane caught the wounded McAllister and strangled him to death, fulfilling his years-long quest for revenge for the murder of his wife and daughter. McAllister is the true identity of the serial killer Red John.

tv_episodes.title: My Blue Heaven
tv_episodes.creator: Tom Szentgyorgyi
tv_episodes.notes: First episode of The Mentalist without any human murder or suspected murder. A dog named Hugo is killed by antagonist Danny Otero, but no human deaths occur.

persons.name: Defiance's Psychic
persons.notes: Cuban conman posing as a psychic for Defiance Schneiderman; name not given in episode

deaths.motive_detail: Abel caught the psychic snooping in his bedroom; a fight broke out and the psychic hit his head and died. Abel then concealed the body by cutting it up and burying it in his rooftop garden.
deaths.notes: Abel claimed the death was accidental during a fight, but his subsequent concealment of the body (dismemberment and burial in rooftop garden) indicates at least reckless culpability.

deaths.cause_detail: Throat cut and shot in the head
Throat cut and shot in the head
Throat cut and shot in the head
Shot by Krystal Markham; death staged to appear as if Perez was killed in a police gunfight

deaths.motive_detail: Avery killed Charles to prevent him from exposing her espionage activities at Cartesian Studios.

deaths.cause_detail: Avery's father killed himself after the family lost everything financially. Method not stated.
Found tied to a chair and covered in blood; exact cause not established in this episode.

persons.name: Rachel
persons.notes: Real name Rachel; went by Marcia at the commune. Fugitive who had been on the run for 27 years after involvement in a bombing.

deaths.motive_detail: Rachel killed Edith to prevent her from turning Rachel in to authorities after Edith found her via a travel blog.
LaRoche was investigating who was stalking former CBI members and triggered a booby trap left by an unknown party.
Bryce hit Dan with a rock during a fight after Dan discovered Bryce had stolen bribe money from the Millman Oil safe.

deaths.notes: An unknown masked intruder broke into the Rigsby home and shot Wayne Rigsby. Linked to the string of attacks on former CBI team members.
Gallery owner shot dead during an art robbery when he refused to surrender a painting. MacKaye (the gang boss) is later charged with the murder.

deaths.motive_detail: Ronaldo was working on an appeal for death-row inmate Luis Cruz and was close to finding the murder weapon; Mae Feinberg hired Pete Koch to bomb him to prevent the truth from coming out.
Pete Koch, the private investigator hired to bomb Ronaldo, was killed to eliminate him as a witness and retrieve the briefcase.
Mae Feinberg shot Sarah Feinberg 14 years prior so that she and Edward Feinberg could be together; an innocent man (Luis Cruz) was convicted for the crime.
deaths.notes: This murder occurred 14 years before the episode's present day. An innocent man, Luis Cruz, was convicted and nearly executed for it.

persons.name: Peter Kilgallen
persons.profession: Concierge / majordomo of the Foragers Club

deaths.motive_detail: Dyer was planting a camera to blackmail Kimura when Madison caught him; he chased and beat her to death to prevent exposure of his blackmail scheme.
Kilgallen was killed because he figured out that Dyer had murdered Madison Price.

deaths.notes: Shot by trafficker Ramon after escaping the human smuggling operation.
Killed by FBI agents after he opened fire on them during pursuit near the border.
Murdered on orders of Mr. Ridley to eliminate a witness; body found by San Diego police.

Elijah Macey was killed on orders from John Acardo; the trigger man delivered the hit via a coat-concealed contract system. Acardo was initially not indicted due to jury tampering by the prosecutor Lydia Faulk.

Multiple corpses found in a freezer in what appears to be an organ harvesting operation; the bodies had been cut open and their hearts removed. These are victims from the human trafficking ring investigated by Cho and Abbott.
